name: Verisk Insurance Data API
what_it_does: Enterprise REST API providing ISO filings, actuarial datasets, property risk scores, fraud detection signals, and catastrophe models used by P&C insurers for underwriting and claims decisioning.
use_cases: Automated property risk scoring during underwriting workflows
Fraud signal enrichment on first-notice-of-loss claims
ISO filing lookup for rate and form compliance
Catastrophe exposure aggregation for reinsurance reporting
not_for: Startups or non-insurance companies — enterprise contracts required
Consumer-facing applications — data licensing prohibits redistribution
Low-latency real-time consumer interactions — designed for batch and transactional underwriting workflows
best_when: A P&C insurer's underwriting or claims platform needs enrichment with authoritative actuarial, property, or fraud data from the industry's primary data aggregator.
avoid_when: You don't have an enterprise insurance industry relationship, need self-service access, or are building outside the P&C insurance vertical.

auth.notes: API key plus mutual TLS (mTLS) for enterprise clients. Some product lines require dedicated VPN or private network connectivity. Auth method varies by specific data product and contract.

pricing.paid_tiers: Annual enterprise contracts; pricing per transaction or per data product license; typically six-figure minimums
pricing.notes: No self-service pricing. Enterprise contracts negotiated directly with Verisk account teams. Minimum commitments and data licensing terms vary significantly by product line.

performance.rate_limits: Defined in enterprise contract; typically based on transaction volume

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Documentation is largely behind enterprise login walls. Error responses vary by product line and reflect legacy system origins. Error codes are often numeric with minimal descriptive content.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Read/lookup operations are idempotent. No explicit idempotency keys for write operations. Designed for transactional insurance workflows where each call is a distinct underwriting event.
known_agent_gotchas: No public API documentation — all docs are behind authenticated enterprise portals
Different data products have different endpoints, auth methods, and schemas — not a unified API
Data licensing terms restrict how responses can be stored, cached, or redistributed
Contract negotiation required before any API access — cannot evaluate without business relationship
Some data products require on-premise or private network connectivity rather than public internet
Rate limits and SLAs vary by specific data product contract
Agent access to actuarial data may require explicit licensing amendment

security_score_components.security_notes: mTLS and VPN requirements reflect enterprise-grade security appropriate for sensitive actuarial and fraud data. However, the security model is perimeter-based rather than modern zero-trust. No fine-grained OAuth scopes — access is all-or-nothing per data product contract.
